macro_segments.title: Tumah of Poletet Shichvat Zera: The Debate Between Chachamim and Rabbi Shimon

micro_segments.title: Introduction to the Issue: Interior vs. Exterior Flow of Semen in Women
The Core Debate: Chachamim Say Interior Like Blood; Rabbi Shimon Says Exterior Like Man
Tosafot's Clarification: Semen Must Travel from Uterus to Vaginal Canal
Gemara's Question: How Can Rabbi Shimon Say Outside If He Says Inside Too?
Resolution: Distinction Between Meshameshet (After Sex) and Poletet (Semen Coming Out)

macro_segments.title: The Practical Concern: Three Days of Possible Emission and the Case of Lying Down

micro_segments.title: Rava's Teaching: Woman Cannot Eat Terumah for Three Days After Sex
Gemara's Problem: How Can She Go to Mikvah If Semen Is Always Coming Out?
Solution: Woman Was Dunked in Mikvah While Lying in Bed Without Standing
Clarification: Rava Speaks of Woman Turning Over While Horizontal vs. Walking
Rashi's Explanation: Different Cases of Immobility and Motion Affect Emission Timing
The Torah's Case: Only When Woman Is Not Thrashing About in Bed

macro_segments.title: Rav Shmuel Bar Bisna's Question: Is Poletet Shichvat Zera a Ro'ah or Noga'at?

micro_segments.title: The Question: Is She Becoming Tamei Because of Her Body (Ro'ah) or From Outside (Noga'at)?
The Implications: Listor (Interrupting Clean Days), Tamei Bifnim Kibachutz, and Minimal Amount
The Obvious Answer From Baraita: Chachamim Say Ro'ah; Rabbi Shimon Implies Noga'at
Logical Answer Without Text: She Should Be Noga'at Since Semen Is External

macro_segments.title: Ma'amad Har Sinai: Evidence That Poletet Is a Ro'ah Status

micro_segments.title: The Question From Ma'amad Har Sinai: Why Was Three Days Required Before Receiving Torah?
Chazal's Answer: Three Days Allows Time for Women to Finish Emitting Semen
The Significance: That Women Had To Be Tahor Shows This Is About Ro'ah Experience
Counter-Argument: Maybe Ma'amad Har Sinai Is Special, Not Proof of Halakhic Category

macro_segments.title: The Amoraic Consensus: All Authorities Rule She Is a Ro'ah

micro_segments.title: Abaye's Answer: She Is a Ro'ah
Rava's Answer: She Is a Ro'ah
Rav Yosef's Answer: She Is a Ro'ah
Back to Abaye With Frustration: All Three Give Same Answer - What Is the Difference?
Abaye's Final Clarification: Rabbi Shimon Requires Exit of Body, But She Is Still a Ro'ah

macro_segments.title: The Metaphor and Implications: 'Spitting the Same Spit' - Discussing Ro'ah Status and Its Consequences

micro_segments.title: The Literary Point: The Metaphor of 'Spitting the Same Spit' Relates to External Liquids Coming Out
The Halakhic Point: If She Is a Ro'ah, She Must Wait Until Day Six to Begin Counting
Ashkenazi Custom: Wait Until Day Six; Sefardi Custom: Can Start Earlier With Ritual Washing

macro_segments.title: The Baraita About Yoledet: When Does a Woman Become Tamei After Childbirth?

micro_segments.title: The Teaching: Niddah, Zavah, Shomeret Yom Kineged Yom, and Yoledet All Cause Tumah Bifnim Kibachutz
The Question: Yoledet Is Not About Flow - How Is She Tamei Inside?
Answer: A Woman Immersing After Her Seven or Fourteen Days Enters Taharah
Case Example: If Blood Emerges Before Mikvah She Is Tamei; If After Mikvah She Is Tahor

macro_segments.title: The Tangled Question of Blood Inside the Body: Tumah Beluah vs. Conducting Tumah

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Yirmiyah's Question: Why Does Blood Still Inside Her Body Make Her Tamei?
Explanation of Two Concepts: Beit Hastarim (Interior Touch) and Beluah (Swallowed/Absorbed)
Rabbi Abin's Answer: Comparison to Dead Bird - But Only When It Exits the Body
Resolution: The Blood Inside Her Body Will Be Tamei When It Exits; Mikvah Doesn't Purify It While Inside

macro_segments.title: The Yoledet and Mikvah: When Does Mikvah Affect the Status of Blood?

micro_segments.title: The Concern: Does Mikvah Purify Blood That Was in the Body During Tumah Days?
Conclusion: Mikvah Does Not Purify Blood Inside Body; Only Prevents Future Blood From Being Tamei
The Baraita Explained: It Only Tells Us Blood Inside Does Not Make Her Tamei While Inside

macro_segments.title: Dry Childbirth: When Exactly Does a Fetus Become Born for Tumah Purposes?

micro_segments.title: The Question: In a Dry Childbirth (No Blood), What Makes the Yoledet Tamei?
The Answer: It's About When the Fetus Emerges - Specifically When Head Exits Prozdor
Rav Oshaya's Teaching: We Are Concerned Fetus Head May Exit Prozdor Even If Not Noticed
Story: Woman Who Heard Fetus Crying on Erev Shabbos But Didn't Birth Until Shabbos
Rava's Conclusion: The Cry Proves Head Had Exited Prozdor on Erev Shabbos

macro_segments.title: The Anatomy and Halakhic Status: Is Prozdor Bifnim (Interior) or Outside the Body?

micro_segments.title: The Debate: Major Question Whether Exiting Prozdor Means Still Interior or Already Exterior
The Problem: All Other Sources Say Birth Is Only When Head Exits the Body
Acharonim's Explanation: Maybe Prozdor Is Very Close to Exit; Head Emerges But Rest Still Inside
Rav Shlomo Zalman Auerbach: Would Be Machmir If Fetus Exited Uterus Before Body on Different Days

macro_segments.title: Beluah vs. Beit Hastarim: The Debate About Interior Vaginal Canal

micro_segments.title: The Question: Is the Vaginal Canal Interior (Beluah) or Beit Hastarim?
The Case: If Dead Animal Tissue Is Placed Inside, Does She Become Tamei for Carrying?
Abaye's Position: The Vaginal Canal Is Beluah (Absorbed/Swallowed)
Rava's Position: The Vaginal Canal Is Beit Hastarim (Interior Hidden Place)
Rava's Proof: The Baraita About Semen Flow Refers to It as Beit Hastarim
Abaye's Counter: Even If Beit Hastarim, Semen Should Still Cause Tumah Unless It's a Gezerat Hakasuv

macro_segments.title: The Throat and Swallowing: Is the Esophagus Beluah or Beit Hastarim?

micro_segments.title: The Question: Is the Esophagus (Makom Nivul) Beluah or Beit Hastarim?
The Case: If Dead Animal Is Placed Down Throat, Does It Cause Tumah?
Abaye's Position: The Throat Is Beluah
Rava's Implied Position: The Throat Is Beit Hastarim (But Offers No Proof)
Abaye's Proof: Baraita Says Dead Bird Does Not Cause Tumah in Throat - Only Place This Makes Sense
Tosafot's Synthesis: Degrees of Depth - Exterior Throat vs. Deep Throat Distinguish the Two Views
Application to Women: Higher in Vaginal Canal May Be Beit Hastarim; Deeper May Be Beluah
Practical Relevance: Critical for Chatzitzah (Interposition) Issues and Contraceptive Devices
